project.name: urban-pulse-platform
project.description: A Python monorepo (uv workspace) for a real-time urban data platform monitoring traffic, weather, and air quality in the Ho Chi Minh City metro region.

nodes.name: api.py
nodes.summary: Quick payload inspector script that fetches a single VietMap Route v3 API response for the Urban Core to Eastern Innovation route, strips verbose fields, and pretty-prints the structure and annotation details.

nodes.name: strip_verbose
nodes.summary: Recursively removes large fields (points, instructions, snapped_waypoints) from nested dicts/lists to make API response structure readable.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the batch processing application. Empty file serving as Python package marker.

nodes.name: bootstrap_cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for running the full medallion bootstrap pipeline (bronze to silver to gold to baseline), with flags to selectively run only silver or gold stages.

nodes.name: _bootstrap_silver
nodes.summary: Backfills silver layer from all bronze data using the Iceberg catalog without recreating the table.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments and orchestrates the bootstrap pipeline, running silver and/or gold stages based on flags.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for batch jobs subpackage. Empty file serving as Python package marker.

nodes.name: baseline_learning.py
nodes.summary: Computes traffic baseline statistics (mean/stddev of duration and heavy ratio per route, day-of-week, and hour-of-day) from the gold Iceberg table using DuckDB, writing results to a baseline Iceberg table for anomaly detection.

nodes.name: _ensure_baseline_table
nodes.summary: Creates the gold namespace and baseline Iceberg table if they do not already exist.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main entry point that reads all gold hourly data, computes per-route baseline statistics with DuckDB SQL, and overwrites the baseline Iceberg table.

nodes.name: bronze_to_silver.py
nodes.summary: Promotes raw bronze Parquet traffic data from MinIO to a cleaned, typed silver Iceberg table. Supports three modes: microbatch (current hour upsert), bootstrap (full backfill with deduplication), and backfill (specific time range).

nodes.name: _ensure_table
nodes.summary: Creates the silver namespace and partitioned Iceberg table (partitioned by day on timestamp_utc) if they do not exist.

nodes.name: _cast_to_arrow_schema
nodes.summary: Casts a raw bronze Parquet Arrow table to the silver schema, handling column renames, type coercion (including string-to-timestamp and tz-naive timestamps), and null fallbacks for congestion fields.

nodes.name: _read_bronze_parquet
nodes.summary: Reads bronze Parquet files from MinIO via s3fs glob pattern, casting each to the silver schema and concatenating into a single Arrow table.

nodes.name: microbatch
nodes.summary: Upserts the current hour's bronze data into the silver Iceberg table using an idempotent overwrite filter on the timestamp window.

nodes.name: bootstrap
nodes.summary: Full backfill that promotes all bronze data to silver, deduplicating against existing silver rows by (route_id, timestamp_utc) before appending.

nodes.name: backfill
nodes.summary: Backfills silver for a specific time range by iterating every hour and overwriting the corresponding silver window from bronze partitions.

nodes.name: silver_to_gold.py
nodes.summary: Aggregates silver traffic data into gold-layer hourly summaries (observation count, avg/p95 duration, congestion ratios) using DuckDB SQL, writing results to a gold Iceberg table. Supports both incremental hourly runs and full bootstrap.

nodes.name: _ensure_gold_table
nodes.summary: Creates the gold namespace and day-partitioned gold Iceberg table if they do not exist.

nodes.name: _scan_to_table
nodes.summary: Converts PyIceberg scan results to Arrow Table, handling both older (pa.Table) and newer (RecordBatchReader) PyIceberg versions.

nodes.name: _aggregate_silver
nodes.summary: Aggregates silver Arrow table into gold schema using DuckDB SQL with hourly grouping, computing observation counts, duration stats, and congestion ratios.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Incremental hourly job that reads the current hour's silver partition, aggregates it, and upserts into the gold Iceberg table.

nodes.name: bootstrap
nodes.summary: Full re-aggregate that scans all silver data into gold Iceberg table, overwriting the entire gold table.

nodes.name: logger.py
nodes.summary: Simple logging wrapper class that provides info, warning, and error methods using Python's standard logging module with a stream handler.

nodes.name: Logger
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around Python's standard logging.Logger that configures a StreamHandler with DEBUG level and timestamp formatting.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Entry point for the batch processing service that registers and serves Prefect flow deployments (microbatch every 5m, hourly-gold every 1h, retrain every 6h, plus manual bootstrap and backfill).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Configures and serves five Prefect flow deployments with their respective schedules and concurrency limit of 3.

nodes.name: pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Defines the medallion batch pipeline as Prefect tasks and flows: microbatch (bronze-to-silver), hourly-gold (silver-to-gold), retrain (baseline + ML trigger), bootstrap (full pipeline), and backfill (time-range silver recovery).

nodes.name: task_trigger_ml_retrain
nodes.summary: Prefect task that triggers ML model retraining via an HTTP POST to the ml-service endpoint.

nodes.name: retrain
nodes.summary: Prefect flow that runs baseline recomputation from gold data, then triggers ML service retrain with dependency ordering.

nodes.name: bootstrap
nodes.summary: Prefect flow that runs full medallion bootstrap sequentially: bronze-to-silver, then silver-to-gold, then baseline learning.

nodes.name: backfill
nodes.summary: Prefect flow for manually backfilling silver data for a missed time range, triggerable from Prefect UI or CLI.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init file that marks the ingestion directory as a Python package.

nodes.name: logger.py
nodes.summary: Simple logging wrapper class providing info, error, and warning methods with a standard StreamHandler formatter. Appears to be a legacy/unused logger since the rest of the ingestion service uses stdlib logging directly.

nodes.name: Logger
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around Python's stdlib logging.Logger that configures a StreamHandler with timestamp formatting. Exposes info(), error(), and warning() convenience methods.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Entry point for the ingestion service. Selects between StdoutPublisher (dry-run mode) and KafkaPublisher based on config, then runs an infinite crawl loop with a 300-second interval between cycles.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Service entry point that initializes the appropriate publisher (stdout or Kafka) based on dry_run setting, then enters an infinite loop calling the orchestrator's run() with a 5-minute sleep between cycles. Ensures publisher.close() on exit.

nodes.name: orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a single ingestion crawl cycle: loads route definitions from a JSON file, sequentially fetches traffic data for each route via the VietMap API with a 10-second inter-request delay, and publishes observations to the configured publisher.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Executes a full crawl cycle: iterates over all configured routes, calls fetch_route for each with error handling per route, publishes results, and sleeps 10s between requests to avoid API rate limits. Logs progress with route index, distance, duration, and API latency.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Publisher protocol (interface) for ingestion event publishing and provides a StdoutPublisher implementation for dry-run mode that outputs newline-delimited JSON to stdout. Also exports the TRAFFIC_TOPIC constant.

nodes.name: Publisher
nodes.summary: Protocol (structural typing interface) defining the contract for ingestion publishers: publish(observation) and close() methods. Used as a dependency injection point for stdout vs Kafka implementations.

nodes.name: StdoutPublisher
nodes.summary: Dry-run publisher that serializes TrafficRouteObservation to newline-delimited JSON (with topic and key metadata) and prints to stdout. Requires no Kafka infrastructure.

nodes.name: kafka.py
nodes.summary: Kafka-backed publisher that produces TrafficRouteObservation messages to the traffic-route-bronze topic with ingest_ts headers via the urbanpulse_infra KafkaProducer client.

nodes.name: KafkaPublisher
nodes.summary: Concrete publisher that wraps urbanpulse_infra.kafka.KafkaProducer to serialize and produce TrafficRouteObservation payloads to Redpanda/Kafka with millisecond ingest timestamps in message headers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init file for the ingestion sources subpackage.

nodes.name: openmeteo.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder/stub file for the Open-Meteo weather data source connector. Currently empty (no implementation).

nodes.name: vietmap.py
nodes.summary: VietMap traffic API source connector. Fetches route directions between origin/destination coordinates, parses congestion annotations from the response, and returns typed TrafficRouteObservation domain objects with congestion metrics.

nodes.name: fetch_route
nodes.summary: Fetches a single route from the VietMap directions API using origin/destination coordinates, parses distance, duration, and congestion segment annotations, and returns a fully typed TrafficRouteObservation with UTC timestamp.

nodes.name: waqi.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder/stub file for the WAQI (World Air Quality Index) data source connector. Currently empty (no implementation).

nodes.name: test_orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder test file for the ingestion orchestrator. Currently contains only a docstring with no test implementations.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the ML app. Empty file that marks the ml directory as a Python package.

nodes.name: api.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI service for triggering and monitoring ML training runs. Exposes /health, /status, and /train endpoints. Uses thread-safe in-memory state to prevent concurrent training and track run status. The /train endpoint synchronously invokes run_training and returns metrics.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the ML evaluation subpackage. Empty marker file.

nodes.name: metrics.py
nodes.summary: Evaluation metrics for unsupervised anomaly detection. Computes proxy metrics (anomaly rates, inter-model agreement, Jaccard similarity) since there are no ground-truth labels. Output is formatted for MLflow logging.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the ML features subpackage. Empty marker file.

nodes.name: traffic_features.py
nodes.summary: Feature engineering pipeline for traffic anomaly detection. Joins gold hourly traffic observations with baseline statistics from Iceberg tables via DuckDB SQL to compute deviation-based features: duration z-score, heavy ratio deviation, p95/mean ratio, observation count, and max severe segments.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the ML models subpackage. Empty marker file.

nodes.name: isolation_forest.py
nodes.summary: Scikit-learn IsolationForest wrapper for multivariate traffic anomaly detection. Handles feature extraction from Arrow tables, NaN/inf sanitization, model training, prediction (converting sklearn's -1/1 labels to 1/0), decision scoring, and parameter export for MLflow.

nodes.name: zscore_detector.py
nodes.summary: Simple threshold-based Z-score anomaly detector. Flags observations as anomalous when the absolute duration z-score exceeds a configurable threshold (default 3.0). No training required -- operates directly on pre-computed feature columns.

nodes.name: train
nodes.summary: POST /train endpoint that synchronously triggers a full ML training run. Uses a threading lock to prevent concurrent training. Returns training status, run ID, sample count, and evaluation metrics. Catches exceptions and updates in-memory state accordingly.

nodes.name: compute_metrics
nodes.summary: Computes evaluation metrics comparing Z-score and IsolationForest detector outputs. Returns anomaly rates for each model, inter-model agreement fraction, and Jaccard similarity of anomaly sets. Handles empty arrays safely.

nodes.name: build_features
nodes.summary: Builds the feature table by loading gold and baseline Iceberg tables, joining them via DuckDB SQL on route_id/day_of_week/hour_of_day, and computing deviation-based features. Returns an Arrow table or None if data is missing.

nodes.name: TrainResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model for the /train endpoint. Contains training status, MLflow run ID, sample count, and evaluation metrics dictionary.

nodes.name: TrainStatus
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model for the /status endpoint. Tracks whether training is currently active, last run ID, status, and start timestamp.

nodes.name: IsolationForestDetector
nodes.summary: Wrapper around sklearn IsolationForest for traffic anomaly detection. Provides fit/predict/decision_scores methods that accept PyArrow Tables, handles feature column extraction and NaN sanitization, converts sklearn label convention to 0/1, and exposes hyperparameters for MLflow logging.

nodes.name: ZScoreDetector
nodes.summary: Threshold-based anomaly detector using pre-computed duration Z-scores. Flags observations exceeding a configurable threshold as anomalies. Stateless -- no training required.

nodes.name: train.py
nodes.summary: ML training pipeline entry point that reads gold and baseline Iceberg tables, engineers features, trains Z-score and IsolationForest anomaly detectors, and logs all parameters, metrics, artifacts, and models to MLflow.

nodes.name: TrainResult
nodes.summary: Simple data class holding the outcome of a training run: run_id, status, sample_count, and a metrics dictionary.

nodes.name: run_training
nodes.summary: Executes the full ML training pipeline: configures MLflow, loads Iceberg catalog, builds features, trains Z-score and IsolationForest detectors, logs dataset/params/metrics/model to MLflow, and returns a TrainResult.

nodes.name: _log_feature_stats
nodes.summary: Computes per-feature distribution statistics (mean, std, percentiles) and logs them as MLflow metrics and a JSON artifact.

nodes.name: _log_anomaly_details
nodes.summary: Builds per-route anomaly summaries from Z-score and IsolationForest labels, collects sample anomalous observations where both models agree, and logs them as JSON artifacts to MLflow.

nodes.label: kafka.py
nodes.summary: Kafka consumer wrapper for the streaming app. Encapsulates confluent_kafka Consumer with subscribe, poll, commit, and stop lifecycle methods. Handles partition EOF and error conditions during polling.

nodes.label: KafkaConsumer
nodes.summary: Wrapper around confluent_kafka.Consumer providing a simplified interface for subscribing to topics, polling messages with error handling (including partition EOF), manual commit, and graceful shutdown.

nodes.label: logger.py
nodes.summary: Simple logging utility for the streaming app. Wraps Python's standard logging module with a Logger class providing info, error, and warning convenience methods with a consistent format.
nodes.name: logger.py

nodes.label: Logger
nodes.summary: Lightweight logging wrapper that configures a named Python logger with INFO level, a StreamHandler, and a timestamp-name-level-message formatter. Provides info(), error(), and warning() convenience methods.

nodes.label: main.py
nodes.summary: Entry point for the streaming service. Sets up signal handlers, creates a Kafka consumer for the traffic-route-bronze topic, dispatches messages to the TrafficProcessor, handles dead-letter queue publishing for failed messages, and performs graceful shutdown with buffer draining.
nodes.name: main.py

nodes.label: main
nodes.summary: Main event loop that creates Kafka consumer and processor instances, polls for messages, dispatches to the appropriate processor, commits offsets on flush, and handles graceful shutdown with buffer draining.
nodes.name: main.py

nodes.label: _publish_to_dlq
nodes.summary: Best-effort publish of a failed Kafka message to a dead-letter topic by appending '-dlq' suffix to the original topic name, forwarding the original value with an error header.

nodes.summary: Empty package init for the streaming processors module.

nodes.label: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract BaseProcessor class that all stream processors must implement, providing the process(), on_error(), flush(), and check_time_flush() interface methods.
nodes.name: base.py

nodes.label: BaseProcessor
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for stream processors. Defines the contract: process() handles a single Kafka message and returns whether a flush occurred; on_error() for custom error handling; flush() for shutdown buffer draining; check_time_flush() for time-based flushing.
nodes.name: base.py

nodes.label: traffic.py
nodes.summary: Implements the TrafficProcessor that consumes traffic-route-bronze Kafka messages, validates them as TrafficRouteObservation, buffers records, and flushes to MinIO as partitioned Parquet files. Includes end-to-end latency tracking and time-based flushing.
nodes.name: traffic.py

nodes.label: TrafficProcessor
nodes.summary: Concrete stream processor for traffic route data. Deserializes Kafka messages into TrafficRouteObservation models, buffers up to 500 records, then flushes as Snappy-compressed Parquet to MinIO bronze layer with time-partitioned paths. Supports both size-based and time-based (30s) flush triggers.
nodes.name: traffic.py

nodes.label: _to_parquet
nodes.summary: Converts a list of TrafficRouteObservation Pydantic models into Snappy-compressed Parquet bytes using PyArrow, extracting all traffic fields including congestion metrics.
nodes.name: traffic.py

nodes.label: weather.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder module for a future weather stream processor. Currently contains only a docstring.

nodes.label: MinioClient

nodes.label: routes_generator.py
nodes.summary: CLI utility that reads zones.json and generates directed inter-zone routes. For each zone, it iterates over its connects_to list and builds route objects with origin/destination names and anchor coordinates, writing the result to routes.json.

nodes.label: Route

nodes.label: generate_routes
nodes.summary: Reads zones.json, builds a lookup by zone ID, then iterates each zone's connects_to list to produce directed Route dicts with origin/destination names and anchor coordinates.

nodes.label: test.py
nodes.summary: Ad-hoc integration test script for the VietMap traffic routing API. Defines HCMC zone anchors, calls the VietMap directions endpoint for every zone pair, calculates congestion ratios from the response, and appends raw results to routes.json. Uses dotenv for API key management.

nodes.label: _get_a_route
nodes.summary: Generator that calls the VietMap route API for a given origin/destination coordinate pair, strips verbose fields (points, instructions), computes congestion ratios, appends raw JSON to routes.json, and yields the response data.

nodes.label: _calc_congestion_for_interval
nodes.summary: Calculates congestion statistics (heavy/moderate/low ratios and severe count) for a given point interval within congestion segment data returned by the VietMap API.
nodes.name: test.py

nodes.label: test_vietmap_api
nodes.summary: Iterates all zone pairs (excluding self-pairs) and calls _get_a_route for each, printing results. Serves as an integration smoke test for the VietMap directions API across all HCMC zones.
nodes.name: test.py

layers.name: Ingestion Service
layers.description: Polls external APIs (VietMap traffic, Open-Meteo weather, WAQI air quality) and publishes raw events to Redpanda topics

layers.name: Streaming Service
layers.description: Consumes Redpanda topics in real time, applies domain-specific processing, and writes raw bronze events to MinIO

layers.name: Batch Processing Service
layers.description: Scheduled DuckDB jobs that promote data through the medallion layers: bronze to silver (cleaning/validation) and silver to gold (aggregation)

layers.name: ML Service
layers.description: Trains anomaly detection models (IsolationForest, Z-score) on gold-layer data, tracks experiments with MLflow, and exposes a prediction API

layers.name: Serving API
layers.description: FastAPI REST service that exposes anomaly and traffic metrics endpoints to external consumers, reading from the DuckDB gold layer

layers.name: Shared Packages
layers.description: Internal shared libraries: Pydantic domain models and config (core), client factories for Kafka/DuckDB/MinIO/MLflow (infra-clients), and Prometheus metrics/logging/tracing stubs (observability)

layers.name: Infrastructure & Scripts
layers.description: Docker/infrastructure shell scripts (MinIO bucket init, Redpanda topic init, Dremio setup, health checks) plus root-level utility scripts (routes_generator.py, api.py, test.py)

tour.title: Platform Overview
tour.description: urban-pulse-platform is a Python monorepo (uv workspace) that ingests real-time traffic, weather, and air quality data for the Ho Chi Minh City metro region. Data flows through a medallion architecture: external APIs → Redpanda → MinIO bronze → silver → gold → ML → FastAPI. Start here to understand the monorepo layout (apps/, packages/, infra/) and the geographic domain model in zones.json and routes.json.
tour.languageLesson: The project uses a uv workspace: each app under apps/ and each package under packages/ is an independent Python project with its own pyproject.toml, all managed by a single workspace root.

tour.title: Domain Models
tour.description: All apps share a vocabulary defined in packages/core. The Pydantic models here — TrafficReading, WeatherReading, AnomalyEvent, and the zone/route geometry — are the lingua franca passed between every layer. Understanding these models first makes every downstream file easier to read.
tour.languageLesson: config.py (fan-in 14 — the most-imported file in the repo) uses Pydantic BaseSettings with environment-variable binding, giving every app a single, typed config object loaded once at startup.

tour.title: Ingestion: External APIs to Kafka
tour.description: The ingestion app polls three external APIs (VietMap traffic, Open-Meteo weather, WAQI air quality) and publishes raw events to Redpanda topics. The orchestrator manages concurrent polling across all configured routes and zones, while the Kafka publisher wraps the infra-clients KafkaProducer with retry logic and structured logging.
tour.languageLesson: The orchestrator uses asyncio.gather across route coroutines — a common Python pattern for I/O-bound fan-out without threads.

tour.title: Streaming: Kafka to Bronze
tour.description: The streaming app continuously consumes Redpanda topics, applies lightweight per-message processors (traffic, weather, air quality), and writes validated JSON events to MinIO as bronze-layer Parquet/JSON files. The base processor class establishes a common interface that all three domain processors implement.
tour.languageLesson: processors/base.py defines an abstract BaseProcessor — a strategy pattern that lets main.py dispatch to the correct handler purely by topic name, keeping the consumer loop generic.

tour.title: Batch Pipeline: Medallion Promotion
tour.description: Scheduled by Prefect, the batch app runs three DuckDB jobs that progressively refine data: bronze_to_silver cleans and validates raw events, silver_to_gold aggregates them into analytics-ready tables, and baseline_learning computes rolling statistical baselines used by the ML layer. pipeline.py orchestrates all three in order.
tour.languageLesson: Each job uses DuckDB's ability to query Parquet files directly from MinIO via the S3 filesystem extension — no separate ETL framework needed, just SQL over object storage.

tour.title: ML Training: Anomaly Detection
tour.description: The ml app trains two complementary anomaly detectors on gold-layer data: an IsolationForest for multivariate outliers and a Z-score detector for univariate signal spikes. train.py (fan-out 16 — the highest in the repo) orchestrates feature engineering, model fitting, evaluation, and MLflow experiment logging. The FastAPI endpoint in api.py lets the platform trigger retraining on demand.
tour.languageLesson: traffic_features.py has fan-in 7 (second highest), meaning every model and evaluation path imports it — a deliberate single point of truth for feature definitions that prevents train/serve skew.

tour.title: Serving API: FastAPI for Consumers
tour.description: The serving app exposes the gold layer and ML results over a FastAPI REST API consumed by Grafana dashboards and external clients. Routers are split by domain (anomalies, metrics, health); services contain business logic that queries DuckDB gold tables; schemas define the API contract independently from the core domain models.
tour.languageLesson: dependencies.py uses FastAPI's Depends() system to inject shared resources (DuckDB connection, config) into route handlers — keeping handlers thin and fully testable in isolation.

tour.title: Infrastructure Clients
tour.description: packages/infra-clients provides thin, factory-pattern wrappers around Kafka/Redpanda, MinIO, DuckDB, Apache Iceberg (via Nessie), and MLflow. Every app imports from here rather than configuring clients directly, ensuring consistent connection settings, retry policies, and teardown across the monorepo.
tour.languageLesson: iceberg.py (fan-in 8) is the second most-imported file — Iceberg catalog access is centralised here so all medallion jobs share the same catalog configuration and transaction semantics.

tour.title: Observability
tour.description: packages/observability provides three thin stubs used across all apps: structured JSON logging (logging.py), Prometheus metric registration (metrics.py), and OpenTelemetry trace initialisation (tracing.py). They are intentionally minimal — wire them up once in each service's startup path and the infra stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki) handles the rest.
tour.languageLesson: Each app has its own logger.py (e.g. apps/batch/src/batch/logger.py) that calls the shared observability setup once at import time, following the 'configure once, use everywhere' logging pattern.
